items.mistake: Passing a long-form DataFrame to heatmap without pivoting to a square matrix
items.fix: Compute `corr = df.select_dtypes('number').corr()` or pivot first, then pass the square matrix to `sns.heatmap`.
items.why: Heatmap expects a 2D grid of values; long tidy data produces a meaningless rectangle unless reshaped.
items.example: Wrong: `sns.heatmap(df)` on 150 rows × 4 columns. Right: `sns.heatmap(df.corr())` on a 4×4 correlation matrix.

items.mistake: Calling figure-level and axes-level functions on the same figure without managing axes
items.fix: Use one style per figure: either `fig, ax = plt.subplots()` + axes-level calls, or a single figure-level function that owns the layout.
items.why: Figure-level functions create their own figure; mixing leads to blank subplots or overwritten plots.
items.example: After `sns.catplot(...)`, do not expect `plt.gca()` to be your catplot axes— use the returned FacetGrid object or start a new figure.

items.mistake: Interpreting correlation heatmap colors as causal relationships
items.fix: Treat heatmaps as association summaries only; confirm with domain knowledge, experiments, or causal methods.
items.why: Correlation measures linear co-movement, not cause; spurious correlations are common in wide feature sets.
items.example: Ice cream sales and drowning deaths correlate in summer— the heatmap cell is red, but neither causes the other.

items.mistake: Using pairplot on dozens of numeric columns
items.fix: Subset to the most relevant features (top variance, model importances) or use `corner=True` and fewer columns.
items.why: A 20-feature pairplot creates 400 scatter panels— unreadable and slow to render.
items.example: Prefer `sns.pairplot(df[['age','income','tenure']], hue='churn')` over passing all 40 numeric columns.
